lesson.menu_title: Where Are We Going -- Future Plans with 'Going To' for the Seoul Trip
lesson.menu_desc: Plan the whole trip -- the flight, the visa, the meetings in Seoul -- with 'going to' -- 32 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 6 -- Where Are We Going -- Future Plans with 'Going To'
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Maria Clara | Lesson 6 -- Future Plans with 'Going To'

listenings.text: Hello, Maria Clara. This is Daniel, from the international office. I am calling about your trip to Seoul in November. Here is the plan. Your flight is going to depart from São Paulo on Monday, the tenth, at nine in the morning. You are going to arrive in Seoul on Tuesday afternoon. Your accommodation is a small hotel near the university, so you are not going to travel far to the conference. Tomorrow, I am going to send the full itinerary to your email. Please remember your passport and your visa. Better safe than sorry. Have a good evening, and see you soon.

Good morning, passengers. This is a boarding announcement for the flight to Seoul. In twenty minutes, we are going to start boarding at Gate Fifteen. Please have your passport and your boarding pass ready in your hand. This is a long flight, so keep your small luggage with you. The departure is on time, at nine o'clock. Thank you for your patience, and have a wonderful trip.

extra_audio.text: First, I am going to apply for my visa at the embassy. Then, I am going to book the flight and the accommodation. On Monday, I am going to depart from São Paulo. On Tuesday, I am going to arrive in Seoul for the conference.

paras: Maria Clara is getting ready for a big trip. In November, she is going to travel to Seoul, in South Korea, for an international conference. This week, she is going to prepare everything, one step at a time. First, she is going to apply for her visa at the Korean embassy. "Better safe than sorry," she says. "I am going to do this early."

Her assistant is going to book the flight and the accommodation near the university. The itinerary is almost ready: the departure is on Monday morning, and the arrival in Seoul is on Tuesday. Maria Clara is not going to take a lot of luggage — only one small suitcase. "In my opinion, it is better to travel light," she says.

In Seoul, Maria Clara is going to meet the rector and present her work at the conference. This time, she is not going to bring an interpreter. She is going to speak English on her own. Every night, her granddaughter Clarinha is going to wait for a video call. "Grandma is going to speak English in Korea!" Clarinha says, and they both laugh.
reading.source: A trip plan written for this course

reading.prompt: What is the main idea of the text?
choices: Maria Clara is in Seoul right now, presenting her work at the conference.
Maria Clara is planning her trip to Seoul in November, and she is going to do many things to get ready.
Maria Clara is not going to travel to Seoul this year.

items: In November, Maria Clara is going to travel to Seoul.
The first paragraph says she is going to travel to Seoul in November.
Maria Clara is going to apply for her visa at the Korean embassy.
The first paragraph says she is going to apply for her visa at the Korean embassy.
Maria Clara is going to take a lot of luggage.
The text says she is not going to take a lot of luggage — only one small suitcase.
In Seoul, Maria Clara is going to speak English on her own, with no interpreter.
The third paragraph says she is not going to bring an interpreter; she is going to speak English on her own.

items.situation: Someone asks: "What are you going to do in November?"
tips: I am going to travel to Seoul for a conference.
am / is / are + going to + base verb

items.situation: "Are you going to apply for a visa?"
tips: Yes, I am. I am going to apply for my visa at the embassy.

items.situation: "Are you going to bring an interpreter?"
tips: No, I am not. I am going to speak English on my own.
Negative: I am not going to...

items.situation: "When is your flight going to depart?"
tips: My flight is going to depart on Monday morning.
is going to for he / she / it / my flight

items.situation: A colleague asks which hotel is better.
tips: In my opinion, the hotel near the university is better.
In my opinion... to give your idea

items.situation: You are not sure you have every document.
tips: I am going to check my passport and my visa. Better safe than sorry.
Better safe than sorry = it is good to be careful
